class.name: VoodooI2CController
class.brief: Implements an Intel LPSS Designware I2C Controller
class.doc: This is the base class from which all implementations of a physical Intel LPSS Designware I2C Controller should inherit from. The members of this class are responsible for low-level interfacing with the physical hardware. For the driver implementing the properiety Designware I2C controller interface, see VoodooI2CControllerDriver.

subclass.name: VoodooI2CPCIController
subclass.brief: Implements a PCI Intel LPSS Designware I2C Controller

subclass.name: VoodooI2CACPIController
subclass.brief: Implements an ACPI Intel LPSS Designware I2C Controller

method.name: probe
method.brief: Implemented to beat Apple's own LPSS kexts
method.doc: Apple has their own Intel LPSS controller kernel extensions which (as far as we can tell) are not compatible with the ones found on PCs. We implement a probe function in order to attach ourselves to the I2C controller before Apple's kext does.

return.doc: A pointer to this instance of VoodooI2CController

argument.name: provider
argument.doc: The provider which we have matched against

argument.name: score
argument.doc: Probe score as specified in the matched personality

argument.name: offset
argument.doc: The offset of the register relative to the controller's base address

method.name: start
method.doc: This function is called after VoodooI2CController::probe and is responsible for allocating the resources needed by the physical controller. This includes initialising system power management and calling VoodooI2CController::publishNub.

argument.name: provider
argument.doc: The provider which we have matched against

method.name: stop
method.doc: This function is called before VoodooI2CController::free and is responsible for deallocating the resources that were allocated in VoodooI2CController::start. This includes stopping system power management and stopping the associated controller nub.

argument.name: provider
argument.doc: The provider which we have matched against

method.name: writeRegister
method.brief: Writes a specified value into a controller register

argument.name: offset
argument.doc: The offset of the register relative to the controller's base address

method.name: mapMemory
method.brief: Maps the controller's memory to a virtual address

method.name: publishNub
method.brief: Publishes a VoodooI2CControllerNub entry into the IORegistry for matching
method.doc: This function instantiates a new VoodooI2CControllerNub object and attaches it to the current VoodooI2CController object. It then starts the nub and calls VoodooI2CControllerNub::registerService to publish the nub and begin the matching process.
